title: Deliciously Smoky Argentinian Bean Sprouts Skewers
description: A smoky and molten Argentinian dish featuring sashimi-cut bean sprouts, perfect for kids and vegetarians alike, this recipe is a twist on traditional asado flavors.
instructions: Preheat grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Thread bean sprouts onto skewers, leaving a small space between each sprout.
In a small bowl, whisk together chimichurri sauce, olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Brush the mixture onto both sides of the bean sprouts.
Grill the skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the sprouts are tender and slightly charred.
Remove the skewers from the grill and squeeze with lemon juice.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional chimichurri sauce if desired.
Let the skewers cool for a few minutes before serving to kids.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
